channel.title: Comments for page "Cleaning Larry"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Cleaning Larry"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My god…I first saw it as an interpreted tale in the CN branch. Everyone commented that they started to VOMIT when they see this tale…Ugh, NOT suitable for reading while having lunch:D, but it's really awesome…
TAKE MY UPVOTE!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: im with deci, the reason shit like Palahniuk's works is that he builds up to the gross conceit, not linger on it as the sole point of his piece, which this one starts doing about halfway through. it stops being gross for gross's sake and starts being boring. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Larry is not machine washable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
gross enough to me, upvote.
however, I think washing machine is very useful

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I myself have little tolerance for scatological and gross-out humor, doubly so for the way it's presented here. Pure shock value for the sake of shock, with zero redeeming qualities about this and is essentially an implausible scenario, given that the Foundation would not be testing Larry in such a way that it'd get anywhere near this point. And on the horror scale, simply being grossed out isn't scary. Being disgusting for the sake of disgusting is one of the worst things I can imagine a story doing.

The idea is funny in theory and a one-off "Hey, what if this happened? Hahaha!" thing, but beyond that is where I draw the line. (Although this tale inspired such a negative reaction in me to write this comment up, so I suppose you never lost your touch on that level Salman.)

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm the sort that can read medical textbooks in all their gory detail over dinner, but this one makes me cringe. Why? Well, ever gotten covered in day old garbage that's been sitting in August sun and has diapers in it? The idea of that smell, but worse makes me shudder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it. I read SCP-1545, but it never said anything about people doing all those disgusting actions inside the costume. Can someone clarify?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think a degree of guro has its place in most horror. The classic monsters (zombies, vampires, ghosts, ghouls, frankenstein, etc.) all mostly play off of our instinctive revulsion towards corpses. "Clean" horror is certainly possible, but it is much more difficult to do well and will (as a general rule) not be as effective as the same concept with a little bit of squick-spice. Fear is a physical reaction as much as it is a mental one; and nothing primes the pump for cerebral horror like a little queasiness in the stomach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with this, and it makes the premise of the article essentially LOLfoundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This sort of thing is about exceding the readers' tolerance levels

personal opinion tl;dr
– hide block

And not only tolerance — I'm a sensitive person, but stuff like Guts horrifies me about as much as an academic journal article about the digestive system, for the same reasons that I found this irritating. Fear and horror are a reaction we experience because there is some degree of something in a story/film/image that we find relatable. Whether that connection is the human body, an object, a mundane activity, etc, a story has to tap into enough personal experience to shake us.

I'm so blindsided by the dumb of this whole premise (even aside from my total lack of horror when it comes to things bloody/fecal/stuff a human body just does) that the sensory overload never has a chance to take hold. I'm just left asking "what— WHY? how? what the fuck purpose would a naked menstrual woman as a test subject serve??" and disappointed that it's even non-canonically linked to an SCP that I quite liked. I know scat horror/guro are their own genre, but this is just the opposite of everything I look for in good horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meh, Mr. Palahniuk wrote it better.

Not bad, but it doesn't work for me personally. This sort of thing is about exceding the readers' tolerance levels, and mine are so high that I went and got a second bowl of chili the first time I saw Human Centipede.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The essence of science is repeatability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is guro.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and now that my idle musing has led to this abomination being created, here's a musing that explains why this wouldn't happen.

The Foundation doesn't need to keep testing Larry nonstop. Hell, we don't even need to keep testing Larry until the people inside die. Learning that Larry can be lethal was important knowledge, yes, and that would have led to Larry being soaked with a liquified slurry of sweat, feces, blood, and urine, yes. But why wouldn't we clean Larry before putting more D-class in?

Well, okay. We'd do it once, to see if Larry's effect is potent enough to cause victims to ignore the fact that they're drenched in a slurry of SFBaU (answer: yes it is). But why, in such a case, would we have people Larry themselves to death again?

Even if we want to interview Larry, there's no reason to have him keep frolicking after. Just grab Larry, pull the two halves apart, and then pull the occupants out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who, in-character, is saying this? The way he talks makes me think that he is a researcher, but I can't fathom why anyone except D-class would clean Larry.

After reading the first paragraph, I thought that this was a 173 tale and "Larry" is the name D-class use to refer to it.

"God forbid we have a dirty Larry." made me smile. The rest is not as good. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was… I was eating when I read this. I will never eat turkey again. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's a look "behind the scenes". Sure, it's gross, but it made me think about all the mundane details of running something like the Foundation. Somebody has to clean up the bodies. Even a totally normal large corporation has a lot of infrastructure. They need cooks, janitors, maintenance people; they need to buy office supplies, keep up repairs on the company vehicles, and negotiate with all the people who supply them. Add to that the need to hire armed guards and scientists, and still stay secret, and you get a lot of bureaucracy. I'd be willing to bet that for every researcher actively working with SCPs, there are five people just keeping the place running.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll never look at the SCP-1545 article the same way ever again…

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adorable. +1 with glee. I had to keep myself from squeeing on the bus at the bit about the boots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was eating when I read this, kept eating regardless.

That's just me though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, God, Jesus, Buddha, God-Emperor, 343, Slenderman…

I feel sick. That's all it did- make me feel sick. Not amuse me, not awaken my imagination like at least the other Nightmare Fuel on this site does (the Bone Hive, for example). It didn't make me think, it just waved an overloaded nappy in my face and laughed as I retched. It just made me feel thoroughly sick and wish that it was gone and away as soon as possible. Like, right now. Or sooner.

In my opinion, this is 'a 9-year-old's booger joke' level of sophistication. And I was in a good mood and everything. -1

But hey, at least I'm not hungry anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I read through this twice before it clicked that it was that bloody llama. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts exactly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehhhhhh…. I wasn't grossed out so much as bored from the constant repetition of "vomit… barf…. shit… vomit." Negative to the one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh dear lord, I've heard bad enough horror stories about the stench from mundane mascot suits. This is a level of fridge logic that I shudder to contemplate >.>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so confused, disgusted, and amused. Maybe because i have a stomach flu, and the smell from my own shit makes this just that more real for me…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was hilarious in the discussion thread for SCP-1545. I don't like it as a separate article on the wiki. Downvoted because not everything humorous in the threads needs to be posted on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We had a few cases where women had their periods while inside the suit.

I get that the idea here is scat horror, but there isn't the pretense of even callous science, here, and that makes it less horrifying. Poo is gross. Ooo. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good lord man…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm the writer the site deserves, but not the one it needs right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why? Why did you have to do this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: GAH

item.title: I said it once, I'll say it again.
item.content:encoded:
Flashbacks to the high school bathrooms.

And no vote.

item.title: AAAAAAAAAAAAAA
item.content:encoded: AAAAAAAAAAAAA

item.title: You should change the title
item.content:encoded:
to The Aristocrats.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Disturbing, but pretty accurate. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-468043/scp-1545#post-1560048

the explanation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Boo! Fight the unjust tyranny of the senior staff! Big red letters for all!

On second thought, that would make reading anything obnoxious. Long live the unjust tyranny of the senior staff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1545. Somewhat exaggerated for the sake of the story, but there you go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: FUCK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Larry the Loving Llama.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…

what is this even about? Is this an existing SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad I wasn't eating when I read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ew ew ew ew ew ew. EW.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take your filthy upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Voct for bringing up the question if somebody cleans Larry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
EEEEEEEEEWWWWWWWWWW

please note that this text size is restricted to senior staff

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say you succeeded in your goal of making it as disgusting as possible, and you indirectly made this the funniest thing I've read today, but beyond that there really isn't much substance to it. So neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Euuuuuuuuurrrrrrgh.

Upvoted.
